evals.name: getsnooz-shopify-pages-heavy
evals.prompt: Replicate the design of the liberated site at /Users/matt/projects/a8c/data-liberation-agent/output/getsnooz.com/. The site is a Shopify storefront for a sleep-products brand. Produce a complete WordPress block theme directory that — once installed and activated — would render the imported WXR content with visual fidelity to the source. Inspect the screenshots in screenshots/desktop/ and the rendered HTML in html/ for at least the homepage and 2-3 other archetype representatives. Use palette.json, typography.json, breakpoints.json as inputs. The output.wxr describes what archetypes are present (run `grep -c '<wp:post_type>page' output.wxr` etc. or use a similar method). Save all generated theme files (style.css, theme.json, functions.php, templates/, parts/, patterns/) into your outputs directory under a sub-folder named getsnooz-replica/. Also save a summary.json at the root of outputs/ describing: archetypes detected, templates created, patterns created, custom blocks created (if any), open questions. Do NOT actually install the theme into Studio — just produce the files. If design-foundation.json is missing, you may either (a) invoke the design-foundations skill to generate it first, or (b) generate a minimal valid foundation inline from palette.json + typography.json by applying simple heuristics (lightest palette entry = surface.base, darkest = text.default, most saturated = accent.primary, most-frequent body fontFamily = typography.families.body).
evals.expected_output: A theme directory `getsnooz-replica/` containing at minimum: style.css with valid theme header, theme.json v3 with palette derived from palette.json (surface, text, accent, border slugs) and fontFamilies derived from typography.json, functions.php (minimal — pattern registration + font enqueue), templates/index.html, templates/page.html, parts/header.html, parts/footer.html, patterns/ for hero + footer + at least one section. summary.json at outputs root. No emojis anywhere in theme files. Pattern PHP headers reference source screenshots.

evals.name: dopplepress-wix-products-mixed
evals.prompt: Replicate the design of the liberated site at /Users/matt/projects/a8c/data-liberation-agent/output/www.dopplepress.com-riso/. The site is a Wix-hosted riso print store with both products (12 in products.jsonl) and content pages. The replica must include WooCommerce-aware templates (single-product.html, archive-product.html) since products are present. Inspect screenshots in screenshots/desktop/ — riso prints have a distinctive aesthetic worth honoring (saturated colors, simple grid layouts). Use palette.json, typography.json, breakpoints.json as design inputs. Look at 1-2 product pages and 1-2 non-product pages. Save all generated theme files into your outputs directory under a sub-folder named dopplepress-riso-replica/. Also save a summary.json describing: archetypes detected, templates created (must include single-product.html and archive-product.html), patterns created (must include product-card and product-hero), custom blocks created (probably none — Woo blocks should suffice), open questions. Do NOT actually install. If design-foundation.json is missing, generate it via the design-foundations skill or inline heuristics as above.
evals.expected_output: A theme directory `dopplepress-riso-replica/` containing: style.css, theme.json v3 (with Woo-friendly settings), functions.php, templates/index.html, templates/page.html, templates/single-product.html, templates/archive-product.html, parts/header.html, parts/footer.html, patterns/ including product-card.php, product-hero.php, hero, footer. summary.json at outputs root. Theme.json colors map to palette.json; fontFamilies map to typography.json.

evals.name: biostratamarketing-hubspot-blog-heavy
evals.prompt: Replicate the design of the liberated site at /Users/matt/projects/a8c/data-liberation-agent/output/www.biostratamarketing.com/. The site is a HubSpot-hosted B2B life-sciences marketing agency with a heavy blog (many `blog--*.html` files). The replica must include strong post templates (single.html, home.html or archive.html) plus generic page templates. Inspect screenshots — this is a corporate/professional aesthetic, sober color palette, lots of long-form content. Use palette.json, typography.json, breakpoints.json. Look at the homepage (find it in manifest.json), 2-3 blog posts, and 1-2 non-blog pages. Save theme files into your outputs directory under biostrata-replica/. Also save a summary.json describing: archetypes detected, templates created (must include single.html and one of home.html/archive.html), patterns created (must include post-card and a hero), custom blocks (probably none), open questions. Do NOT actually install. If design-foundation.json is missing, generate it via the design-foundations skill or inline heuristics as above.
evals.expected_output: A theme directory `biostrata-replica/` containing: style.css, theme.json v3, functions.php, templates/index.html, templates/single.html, templates/page.html, templates/home.html OR archive.html (one or the other), parts/header.html, parts/footer.html, patterns/ including post-card.php, hero, footer. theme.json with palette and fontFamilies derived from inputs. summary.json at outputs root.
